Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MOHMADIRFAN SHEIKH's Motion For Leave To File And Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion for leave to file an amended complaint" is granted.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Aug-24-2021 Continued To Oct-20-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Jamila and Sameer Mirzada's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The complaint is uncertain in that it fails to specify the date and location of the accident, among other things. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 430.10(f).) Defendants' demurrer on statute of limitations grounds is overruled. It cannot be determined from the face of the complaint whether Plaintiff's cause of action is time-barred. Plaintiff concedes in his opposition that the accident took place on January 25, 2019, and the complaint was not filed until February 1, 2021, more than two years later. However, Emergency Rule of Court 9 tolled all statutes of limitations and repose for civil causes of action that exceed 180 days from April 6, 2020, until October 1, 2020. Although venue appears to be improper, improper venue is not a ground for demurrer; defendants may file a motion to change venue.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JAMILA MIRZADA and SAMEER MIRZADA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. Defendants failed to meet and confer before filing the motion, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5. In any event, improper venue is not grounds for a motion to strike.
